name: Animation.Pack

values.name: add
values.comment:
Add a new Animation.State to the pack.

You need one animation state per entity you want to animate.
values.type: id -> List Property -> Pack id msg -> Pack id msg

values.name: animate
values.comment:
Start an animation using `Animation.interrupt`

In your update function, it will look something like this:

```
    StartAnimation ->
        let
            newStyles =
                model.styles
                    |> Animation.Pack.animate MyStyle
                        [ Animation.to
                            [ Animation.left (px 0.0)
                            , Animation.opacity 1.0
                            ]
                        ]

        in
            ( { model | styles = newStyles }
            , Cmd.none
            )
```
values.type: id -> List (Step msg) -> Pack id msg -> Pack id msg

values.name: animated
values.comment:
An alternate way to render an animation.

Here's what it looks like

```
    (div |> Animation.Pack.animated model.style MyStyle)
        [ style
            [ ( "position", "absolute" )
            , ( "border-style", "dotted" )
            ]
        ]
        [ text "My Animated Div" ]

```
values.type: Pack id msg -> id -> (List (Attribute msg) -> List (Html msg) -> Html msg) -> List (Attribute msg) -> List (Html msg) -> Html msg

values.name: init
values.type: List ( id, List Property ) -> Pack id msg

values.name: queue
values.comment: Same as `animate` except use `Animation.queue` instead of `Animation.interrupt`.
values.type: id -> List (Step msg) -> Pack id msg -> Pack id msg

values.name: remove
values.comment: Remove an animation state if it's no longer needed for rendering.
values.type: id -> Pack id msg -> Pack id msg

values.name: render
values.type: Pack id msg -> id -> List (Attribute msg)

values.name: subscription
values.comment:
You need to add a subscription to your `Animation.Pack` in order for animations to work.

It generally looks something like this:

```
    , subscriptions = (\model -> Animation.Pack.subscription Animate model.styles)
```
values.type: (Msg -> msg) -> Pack id msg -> Sub msg

values.name: update
values.type: Msg -> Pack id msg -> ( Pack id msg, Cmd msg )
